steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content:
The first three terms $x^4 + 12ax^2 + 36a^2$ form a perfect square: $(x^2 + 6a)^2$. So the full expression is:
$$(x^2 + 6a)^2 - 25 = (x^2 + 6a)^2 - 5^2$$
Apply difference of squares $A^2 - B^2 = (A-B)(A+B)$:
$$(x^2 + 6a - 5)(x^2 + 6a + 5)$$
The two factors are $x^2 + 6a - 5$ and $x^2 + 6a + 5$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$x^2 + 6a + 5$’: matches $x^2 + 6a + 5$ exactly. Correct
  • Choice B — ‘$x^2 - 6a - 5$’: has $-6a$ instead of $+6a$ - wrong sign. Incorrect
  • Choice C — ‘$x^2 + 12a$’: not produced by this factorization. Incorrect
  • Choice D — ‘$x^2 - 5a$’: mixes the constants in a way that doesn't divide the expression. Incorrect
That confirms A is correct.
